items.stem: Yesterday, Wei Ming ___ to the library.
items.rationale: 'Yesterday' shows the past, so use 'walked'.

items.stem: Look! The baby ___ now.
items.rationale: 'now' shows it is happening at this moment, so use 'is sleeping'.

items.stem: Every morning, Siti ___ her teeth.
items.rationale: 'Every morning' shows a habit, so use the present tense 'brushes'.

items.stem: Last week, we ___ to Malaysia.
items.rationale: 'Last week' shows the past; the past tense of 'go' is 'went'.

items.stem: Tomorrow, I ___ visit my grandmother.
items.rationale: 'Tomorrow' shows the future, so use 'will visit'.

items.stem: The boys ___ football at the field right now.
items.rationale: 'right now' shows it is happening, so use 'are playing'.

items.stem: She ___ her homework an hour ago.
items.rationale: 'an hour ago' shows the past, so use 'finished'.

items.stem: Water ___ at 100 degrees.
items.rationale: A fact that is always true uses the present tense 'boils'.

items.stem: He ___ his leg during the match last Saturday.
items.rationale: 'last Saturday' shows the past; the past tense of 'hurt' is 'hurt'.

items.stem: Listen! Someone ___ at the door.
items.rationale: 'Listen!' shows it is happening now, so use 'is knocking'.

items.stem: We ___ our grandparents every Sunday.
items.rationale: 'every Sunday' shows a habit, so use the present tense 'visit'.

items.stem: The sun ___ in the east.
items.rationale: A fact that is always true uses the present tense 'rises'.

items.stem: Ravi ___ his keys yesterday.
items.rationale: 'yesterday' shows the past; the past tense of 'lose' is 'lost'.

items.stem: I ___ my project by next Friday.
items.rationale: 'by next Friday' shows the future, so use 'will finish'.

items.stem: The children ___ in the pool at the moment.
items.rationale: 'at the moment' shows it is happening now, so use 'are swimming'.

items.stem: My father ___ to work by bus every day.
items.rationale: 'every day' shows a habit; a singular subject takes 'goes'.

items.stem: She ___ a beautiful song at the concert last night.
items.rationale: 'last night' shows the past; the past tense of 'sing' is 'sang'.

items.stem: They ___ their dinner now, so please wait.
items.rationale: 'now' shows it is happening, so use 'are eating'.

items.stem: We ___ the museum last month.
items.rationale: 'last month' shows the past, so use 'visited'.

items.stem: He always ___ his bag before school.
items.rationale: 'always' shows a habit; a singular subject takes 'packs'.

items.stem: The team ___ the trophy last year.
items.rationale: 'last year' shows the past; the past tense of 'win' is 'won'.

items.stem: Next holiday, we ___ to Japan.
items.rationale: 'Next holiday' shows the future, so use 'will fly'.

items.stem: The kettle ___ now; please switch it off.
items.rationale: 'now' shows it is happening at this moment, so use 'is boiling'.

items.stem: Long ago, dinosaurs ___ the Earth.
items.rationale: 'Long ago' shows the past, so use 'roamed'.

GR-tenses-basic.iCanStatement: I can choose the right tense for when something happens.
GR-tenses-basic.explanation: The tense shows when something happens. Use the past tense for what already happened (walked, went), the present tense for habits and facts (walks, boils), and 'will' for the future. When something is happening right now, use 'is/are' + verb-ing (is sleeping).
